title: KMT Chairwoman Cheng Li-wun Arrives in Shanghai — 'Journey for Peace' Begins; First KMT Chair China Visit in ~10 Years
detail: KMT Chairwoman Cheng Li-wun and a KMT delegation arrived in Shanghai at noon local time on April 7, 2026, kicking off a high-stakes April 7–12 mainland visit that is the first by a sitting KMT party chair in approximately a decade. Xinhua reported Cheng called the trip a 'journey for peace.' The itinerary includes stops in Jiangsu Province, Shanghai, and Beijing, with the Sun Yat-sen Mausoleum in Nanjing scheduled for April 8 — a venue chosen by Beijing to position the KMT within a Chinese national heritage frame. A confirmed meeting with President Xi Jinping in Beijing remains the trip's political centerpiece. The visit is unfolding 37 days before the planned Trump-Xi Beijing summit (May 14–15), a timing that Taiwan's DPP government and US national security officials have flagged as a deliberate Beijing diplomatic maneuver. Focus Taiwan noted that reaction in Taiwan was mixed: DPP officials described the visit as creating an 'illusion of unification momentum,' while internal KMT divisions — documented by Taiwan News on April 6 — continued, with party members split between viewing the trip as a legitimate peace initiative versus an exercise that risks being exploited by Beijing for propaganda. The Washington Times reported Cheng's stated goal of 'extending kindness, building mutual trust, and ushering in a new spring in cross-strait relations.'

media.caption: KMT chair Cheng Li-wun arrives in Shanghai on April 7 for controversial 'peace tour' of mainland China

title: PLA 1 Aircraft Sortie + 8 PLAN Vessels Around Taiwan April 7 — Continued Low-Key Presence on Day of KMT Chair Arrival
detail: Taiwan's Ministry of National Defense reported (as of 0600 local time April 7, 2026) detecting 1 PLA aircraft sortie entering Taiwan's southwestern ADIZ, alongside 8 PLAN vessels and 1 official ship operating around Taiwan in multiple vectors — with Chinese warships positioned off Taiwan's east coast and in northern, northwestern, and southwestern waters. ANI News reported that Taiwan deployed aircraft, naval ships, and coastal missile systems in response. The sustained multi-vessel, minimal-air posture on the day of KMT Chairwoman Cheng Li-wun's arrival in Shanghai is consistent with PLA 'combined pressure-and-dialogue signaling': maintaining visible naval presence while keeping air activity at a non-provocative single-sortie level to avoid overshadowing the diplomatic channel represented by the KMT visit. Monthly running totals as of April 7: 205 PLA aircraft tracks and 294 ship tracks in April 2026. The 1-sortie figure compares with 3 on April 5, 2 on April 3, and the late-March spike peak of 19 (March 29). YTD 2026 sorties remain approximately 46.5% below the same 2025 period.
